topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery hummed with the usual polite murmur. I stood near a marble pillar, nursing a lukewarm glass of white wine, my gaze drifting over the canvases. One of them, a large, abstract piece I’d labored over for months, hung prominently on the main wall. It was startlingly vibrant, a chaotic explosion of color that felt entirely alien in this hushed, reverent space. I watched a couple of patrons point, their murmurs sounding like background static. A slight tightening in my jaw was the only reaction I permitted. I took a slow sip, letting the ambient noise wash over me, registering nothing more than the temperature of the glass against my palm.

[story 2]

"It’s quite striking, isn't it?" a voice drifted over from a nearby cluster of admirers. I didn't turn my head immediately. My eyes remained fixed on the texture of the carpet beneath my worn boots. When I finally shifted, my expression was carefully neutral, a blank slate. The painting in question dominated the view, its aggressive lines seeming to mock the quiet dignity of the room. I felt a peculiar hollowness settle in my chest, a sensation like watching a distant, uninteresting storm. I adjusted the cuff of my jacket, a small, repetitive motion that anchored me to the present, acknowledging the intrusion without granting it any weight.

[story 3]

They had wheeled my work into the spotlight. The sheer audacity of the display was almost amusing, if one could find amusement in the act of theft. I leaned against a doorway, crossing my arms over my chest. The light caught the edges of the canvas, making the pigments shimmer with an intensity I hadn't felt in a long time. I observed the crowd—their rapt attention, their hushed approval—and felt a distinct lack of engagement. My thoughts moved through the space without destination, like water flowing around stones. I simply stood there, letting the spectacle unfold, my posture suggesting a profound detachment from the significance of the moment.
